content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

All four amendments verified against the codebase. Three identify concrete correctness/rendering bugs (misplaced period in contract identifier, bullet-wrapping free-form markdown, incomplete contract propagation), and one identifies a real dead-code gap where a contract section can never carry production data. All accepted.

## Votes

- `fr-1-617368af`: Accept
  Verified at task_prompt_contract.rs:63. The format string `"{BEAD_TASK_PROMPT_CONTRACT_NAME}/{}.\`"` places the period inside the backtick code span, producing `bead_execution_prompt/1.` as the rendered identifier. Since `prompt_uses_contract()` at line 53 uses exact line matching to detect the contract marker, any downstream consumer or parser extracting the identifier from this guidance text would capture the trailing period as part of the name. Moving the period outside the backtick is a low-risk, high-correctness fix.
- `fr-1-74a3f619`: Accept
  Verified: `already_planned_elsewhere` is hardcoded to `Vec::new()` at cli/project.rs:340, and the bead data model has no field to source it from. The rendering in service.rs falls through to the default message every time. This means the `Already Planned Elsewhere` contract section — which `stage_consumer_guidance()` advertises as authoritative for scope decisions — can never carry real project data in production. The amendment correctly identifies this as a gap that should be either wired or explicitly documented as a placeholder.
- `fr-1-60ee64b1`: Accept
  Verified across all cited locations. The contract guidance block (`task_prompt_contract`) is only injected in `build_stage_prompt()` via engine.rs. Prompt review (prompt_review.rs) rewrites prompts through templates that have no `{{task_prompt_contract}}` placeholder, meaning a rewrite could drop the exact marker line and silently disable contract detection for all downstream stages. Final review (final_review.rs:1577-1592) and completion (completion.rs:248-256) pass raw project prompts without the contract framing, so reviewers and completers judge scope without the canonical Must-Do Scope / Non-Goals / Already Planned Elsewhere sections. This is a real end-to-end integrity gap — the contract exists but isn't consistently propagated to all decision-making stages.
- `fr-1-1646d5c1`: Accept
  Verified: service.rs:564 wraps agents_guidance with `format!("- {guidance}")`, but bundle.rs:951 renders the same field verbatim with `writeln!(out, "{guidance}")`. Since `agents_guidance` is `Option<String>` (free-form markdown), multi-line content — headings, code fences, nested lists — will be mangled into a single malformed bullet item. This is a concrete rendering bug: repo-specific execution instructions could be hidden or misinterpreted. The fix is to render the guidance verbatim, consistent with how bundle.rs already handles it.
